title: Ischnosiphon obliquus

This family is part of the order Aquifoliales, a grouping of flowering plants that are primarily evergreen and woody. intro: Ischnosiphon obliquus, also known as "Raiatedor" or "Titi", is a plant species belonging to the family Stemonuraceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, Ischnosiphon obliquus has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ischnosiphon obliquus is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as to aid in digestion and reduce fever.
